description: CSS styling language completion data for web design

keywords.description: color - Sets the text color of an element. Accepts various formats: named colors, hex codes, RGB, HSL, and more.
keywords.example: color: #ff0000; /* red */ color: rgb(255, 0, 0); color: blue;

keywords.description: background-color - Sets the background color of an element. Part of the background shorthand property.
margin - Sets the outer spacing around an element. Can be set for all sides or individually (top, right, bottom, left).
padding - Sets the inner spacing within an element between the content and border. Follows same syntax as margin.
border - Shorthand property for setting border width, style, and color. Can be applied to all sides or individually.
keywords.example: border: 1px solid black; border-top: 2px dashed red;

keywords.description: width - Sets the width of an element. Can use pixels, percentages, viewport units, or other CSS units.
height - Sets the height of an element. Similar units to width, but also supports min-height and max-height.
display - Controls how an element is displayed and its layout behavior. Key property for modern CSS layouts.
keywords.example: display: block; display: inline; display: flex; display: grid; display: none;

keywords.description: position - Sets the positioning method for an element. Works with top, right, bottom, left properties.
keywords.example: position: relative; position: absolute; position: fixed; position: sticky;

keywords.description: flex - Shorthand for flex-grow, flex-shrink, and flex-basis. Used in flexbox layouts for flexible sizing.
grid - Shorthand for grid-template properties. Defines grid template areas, rows, and columns.
font-size - Sets the size of the font. Can use absolute units (px, pt) or relative units (em, rem, %).
font-family - Specifies font family for text. Provide fallbacks for better cross-platform compatibility.
keywords.example: font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-family: 'Times New Roman', serif;

keywords.description: text-align - Sets horizontal alignment of text content within its container.
keywords.example: text-align: left; text-align: center; text-align: right; text-align: justify;

keywords.description: opacity - Sets the transparency level of an element. Value between 0 (fully transparent) and 1 (fully opaque).
z-index - Controls the vertical stacking order of elements. Higher values appear in front.
top - Sets the top edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
right - Sets the right edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
bottom - Sets the bottom edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
left - Sets the left edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
float - Places an element on the left or right side of its container, allowing text to wrap around it.
keywords.example: float: left; float: right; float: none;

keywords.description: clear - Specifies which sides of an element where floating elements are not allowed.
keywords.example: clear: both; clear: left; clear: right; clear: none;

keywords.description: overflow - Controls what happens when content overflows an element's box.
keywords.example: overflow: hidden; overflow: scroll; overflow: auto; overflow: visible;

keywords.description: overflow-x - Controls horizontal overflow behavior specifically.
keywords.example: overflow-x: hidden; overflow-x: scroll; overflow-x: auto;

keywords.description: overflow-y - Controls vertical overflow behavior specifically.
keywords.example: overflow-y: hidden; overflow-y: scroll; overflow-y: auto;

keywords.description: visibility - Controls whether an element is visible or hidden while maintaining its layout space.
keywords.example: visibility: visible; visibility: hidden; visibility: collapse;

keywords.description: clip-path - Creates a clipping region to show only part of an element.
transform - Applies 2D or 3D transformations to elements like rotation, scaling, translation.
keywords.example: transform: rotate(45deg); transform: scale(1.2); transform: translate(10px, 20px);

keywords.description: transform-origin - Sets the origin point for transformations.
keywords.example: transform-origin: center; transform-origin: top left; transform-origin: 50% 50%;

keywords.description: transition - Shorthand for transition properties to create smooth animations between property changes.
keywords.example: transition: all 0.3s ease; transition: opacity 1s linear;

keywords.description: transition-property - Specifies which CSS properties should transition.
transition-duration - Sets the duration of transition effects.
transition-timing-function - Sets the speed curve of transition effects.
transition-delay - Sets when the transition effect starts.
animation - Shorthand for animation properties to create keyframe animations.
keywords.example: animation: slideIn 1s ease-in-out; animation: spin 2s linear infinite;

keywords.description: animation-name - Specifies the name of the @keyframes rule to use.
animation-duration - Sets how long an animation takes to complete.
animation-timing-function - Sets the speed curve of the animation.
animation-delay - Sets when the animation starts.
animation-iteration-count - Sets how many times an animation repeats.
animation-direction - Sets whether an animation plays forward, backward, or alternates.
keywords.example: animation-direction: normal; animation-direction: reverse; animation-direction: alternate;

keywords.description: animation-fill-mode - Sets what styles are applied before/after the animation executes.
keywords.example: animation-fill-mode: forwards; animation-fill-mode: backwards; animation-fill-mode: both;

keywords.description: animation-play-state - Sets whether an animation is running or paused.
font-weight - Sets the weight (boldness) of the font.
keywords.example: font-weight: normal; font-weight: bold; font-weight: 400; font-weight: 700;

keywords.description: font-style - Sets the style of the font (normal, italic, oblique).
keywords.example: font-style: normal; font-style: italic; font-style: oblique;

keywords.description: font-variant - Sets whether to display text in small caps or normal.
line-height - Sets the height of lines of text, controlling vertical spacing.
letter-spacing - Sets the spacing between characters in text.
word-spacing - Sets the spacing between words in text.
text-decoration - Sets decorations on text like underline, overline, line-through.
keywords.example: text-decoration: none; text-decoration: underline; text-decoration: line-through;

keywords.description: text-transform - Controls capitalization of text.
keywords.example: text-transform: uppercase; text-transform: lowercase; text-transform: capitalize;

keywords.description: text-indent - Sets the indentation of the first line of text in a block.
text-shadow - Adds shadow effects to text.
white-space - Controls how white space inside an element is handled.
keywords.example: white-space: normal; white-space: nowrap; white-space: pre; white-space: pre-wrap;

keywords.description: word-wrap - Allows long words to be broken and wrapped to the next line.
word-break - Sets how words should break when reaching the end of a line.
keywords.example: word-break: normal; word-break: break-all; word-break: keep-all;

keywords.description: background - Shorthand property for all background properties.
keywords.example: background: url('image.jpg') no-repeat center/cover; background: linear-gradient(45deg, red, blue);

keywords.description: background-image - Sets background images for an element.
keywords.example: background-image: url('bg.jpg'); background-image: linear-gradient(to right, red, blue);

keywords.description: background-repeat - Sets how background images are repeated.
keywords.example: background-repeat: no-repeat; background-repeat: repeat-x; background-repeat: repeat-y;

keywords.description: background-position - Sets the starting position of background images.
keywords.example: background-position: center; background-position: top right; background-position: 50% 25%;

keywords.description: background-size - Sets the size of background images.
background-attachment - Sets whether background images are fixed or scroll with the page.
keywords.example: background-attachment: scroll; background-attachment: fixed; background-attachment: local;

keywords.description: background-origin - Sets the origin of the background positioning area.
keywords.example: background-origin: border-box; background-origin: padding-box; background-origin: content-box;

keywords.description: background-clip - Sets the painting area of the background.
keywords.example: background-clip: border-box; background-clip: padding-box; background-clip: content-box;

keywords.description: border-width - Sets the width of borders. Can be set for all sides or individually.
border-style - Sets the style of borders (solid, dashed, dotted, etc.).
keywords.example: border-style: solid; border-style: dashed; border-style: dotted; border-style: double;

keywords.description: border-color - Sets the color of borders. Can be set for all sides or individually.
border-radius - Sets rounded corners on elements. Can create circles and complex shapes.
border-top - Shorthand for top border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-top: 2px solid red; border-top: 1px dashed blue;

keywords.description: border-right - Shorthand for right border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-right: 2px solid green; border-right: 1px dotted yellow;

keywords.description: border-bottom - Shorthand for bottom border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-bottom: 2px solid purple; border-bottom: 1px groove orange;

keywords.description: border-left - Shorthand for left border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-left: 2px solid pink; border-left: 1px ridge cyan;

keywords.description: outline - Sets outline around elements (outside borders). Used for focus indicators.
keywords.example: outline: 2px solid blue; outline: none; outline: 1px dotted red;

keywords.description: outline-offset - Sets space between outline and border/edge of element.
box-shadow - Adds shadow effects around elements. Can create depth and layering.
text-overflow - Sets how overflowed content that is not displayed should be signaled.
vertical-align - Sets vertical alignment of inline or table-cell elements.
keywords.example: vertical-align: top; vertical-align: middle; vertical-align: baseline;

keywords.description: list-style - Shorthand for list-style-type, list-style-position, and list-style-image.
keywords.example: list-style: none; list-style: disc inside; list-style: square outside;

keywords.description: list-style-type - Sets the type of list-item marker.
keywords.example: list-style-type: disc; list-style-type: decimal; list-style-type: none;

keywords.description: list-style-position - Sets the position of list-item markers.
list-style-image - Sets an image as list-item marker.
cursor - Sets the type of cursor to display when hovering over an element.
keywords.example: cursor: pointer; cursor: grab; cursor: not-allowed; cursor: text;

keywords.description: pointer-events - Controls whether an element can be the target of pointer events.
keywords.example: pointer-events: auto; pointer-events: none; pointer-events: all;

keywords.description: user-select - Controls whether text can be selected by the user.
keywords.example: user-select: none; user-select: auto; user-select: text; user-select: all;

keywords.description: resize - Sets whether an element is resizable by the user.
keywords.example: resize: none; resize: both; resize: horizontal; resize: vertical;

keywords.description: min-width - Sets the minimum width of an element.
max-width - Sets the maximum width of an element.
min-height - Sets the minimum height of an element.
max-height - Sets the maximum height of an element.
box-sizing - Sets how the total width and height of an element is calculated.
margin-top - Sets the top margin of an element.
margin-right - Sets the right margin of an element.
margin-bottom - Sets the bottom margin of an element.
margin-left - Sets the left margin of an element.
padding-top - Sets the top padding of an element.
padding-right - Sets the right padding of an element.
padding-bottom - Sets the bottom padding of an element.
padding-left - Sets the left padding of an element.
flex-direction - Sets the direction of flex items in a flex container.
keywords.example: flex-direction: row; flex-direction: column; flex-direction: row-reverse;

keywords.description: flex-wrap - Sets whether flex items wrap to new lines.
keywords.example: flex-wrap: nowrap; flex-wrap: wrap; flex-wrap: wrap-reverse;

keywords.description: flex-flow - Shorthand for flex-direction and flex-wrap.
keywords.example: flex-flow: row wrap; flex-flow: column nowrap;

keywords.description: justify-content - Aligns flex items along the main axis.
keywords.example: justify-content: center; justify-content: space-between; justify-content: flex-start;

keywords.description: align-items - Aligns flex items along the cross axis.
keywords.example: align-items: center; align-items: stretch; align-items: flex-end;

keywords.description: align-content - Aligns wrapped flex lines.
keywords.example: align-content: center; align-content: space-around; align-content: stretch;

keywords.description: align-self - Overrides align-items for individual flex items.
keywords.example: align-self: auto; align-self: center; align-self: flex-end;

keywords.description: flex-grow - Sets how much a flex item should grow relative to others.
flex-shrink - Sets how much a flex item should shrink relative to others.
flex-basis - Sets the initial main size of a flex item before free space is distributed.
order - Sets the order of flex or grid items without changing HTML structure.
gap - Shorthand for row-gap and column-gap in grid and flexbox layouts.
row-gap - Sets the gap between rows in grid and flexbox layouts.
column-gap - Sets the gap between columns in grid and flexbox layouts.
grid-template-columns - Defines the size and names of grid columns.
grid-template-rows - Defines the size and names of grid rows.
grid-template-areas - Defines grid areas by referencing grid area names.
grid-area - Shorthand for grid-row-start, grid-column-start, grid-row-end, grid-column-end.
grid-column - Shorthand for grid-column-start and grid-column-end.
grid-row - Shorthand for grid-row-start and grid-row-end.
grid-auto-columns - Sets the size of implicitly created grid columns.
grid-auto-rows - Sets the size of implicitly created grid rows.
grid-auto-flow - Controls how auto-placed items get inserted in the grid.
keywords.example: grid-auto-flow: row; grid-auto-flow: column; grid-auto-flow: dense;

keywords.description: place-items - Shorthand for align-items and justify-items in grid layouts.
place-content - Shorthand for align-content and justify-content in grid layouts.
place-self - Shorthand for align-self and justify-self in grid layouts.
filter - Applies graphical effects like blur, brightness, contrast to elements.
keywords.example: filter: blur(5px); filter: brightness(150%); filter: drop-shadow(2px 2px 4px black);

keywords.description: backdrop-filter - Applies graphical effects to the area behind an element.
will-change - Hints to browsers about what properties will change to optimize performance.
keywords.example: will-change: transform; will-change: opacity; will-change: auto;

keywords.description: content - Used with ::before and ::after pseudo-elements to insert content.
counter-increment - Increments one or more CSS counters.
counter-reset - Creates or resets one or more CSS counters.
quotes - Sets quotation marks for different levels of nested quotations.
object-fit - Sets how replaced elements (img, video) should be resized to fit their container.
keywords.example: object-fit: cover; object-fit: contain; object-fit: fill;

keywords.description: object-position - Sets the position of replaced elements within their container.
keywords.example: object-position: center; object-position: top right; object-position: 25% 75%;

keywords.description: aspect-ratio - Sets a preferred aspect ratio for the box.
container - Shorthand for container-type and container-name. Defines a containment context for container queries.
container-type - Declares which axes of the element establish a containment context (normal, size, inline-size).
container-name - Names a containment context for targeted container queries.
inset - Shorthand for top, right, bottom, left. Sets all four offsets at once.
inset-block - Logical shorthand for block-start and block-end offsets (top/bottom in horizontal writing mode).
inset-inline - Logical shorthand for inline-start and inline-end offsets (left/right in horizontal LTR).
margin-block - Logical shorthand setting block-start and block-end margins. Respects writing mode.
margin-inline - Logical shorthand setting inline-start and inline-end margins. Replaces margin-left/right in flow-relative layouts.
padding-block - Logical shorthand for block-start and block-end padding.
padding-inline - Logical shorthand for inline-start and inline-end padding.
border-block - Logical shorthand for block-start and block-end borders.
border-inline - Logical shorthand for inline-start and inline-end borders.
block-size - Logical equivalent of height (in horizontal writing mode). Respects writing direction.
inline-size - Logical equivalent of width (in horizontal writing mode). Respects writing direction.
min-block-size - Logical equivalent of min-height.
min-inline-size - Logical equivalent of min-width.
max-block-size - Logical equivalent of max-height.
max-inline-size - Logical equivalent of max-width. Ideal for readable line lengths.
scroll-behavior - Controls whether scrolling snaps instantly or animates smoothly (auto | smooth).
scroll-snap-type - Defines snap strictness and axis for a scroll container (x/y/both mandatory/proximity).
scroll-snap-align - Sets the snap alignment of a child within a scroll snap container (start, center, end).
scroll-margin - Outset from the element used for snap targeting. Prevents content hiding behind sticky headers.
scroll-padding - Inset on the scroll container for snap targeting. Offsets the snap viewport.
overscroll-behavior - Controls what happens when scroll reaches boundary (auto, contain, none). Prevents scroll chaining.
text-wrap - Controls how text wraps. Values: wrap, nowrap, balance (for headings), pretty (for paragraphs).
text-underline-offset - Sets the distance of the underline from the text baseline.
writing-mode - Sets whether text flows horizontally or vertically (horizontal-tb, vertical-rl, vertical-lr).
direction - Sets the text direction: ltr (left-to-right) or rtl (right-to-left).
hyphens - Controls automatic hyphenation. Values: none, manual, auto. Requires lang attribute.
overflow-wrap - Allows long words to be broken to prevent overflow (normal, break-word, anywhere).
tab-size - Sets the width of tab characters in elements like <pre> and <code>.
text-rendering - Hints to the rendering engine about what to optimize (speed, legibility, geometricPrecision).
font-display - Controls how a web font is displayed during loading (auto, block, swap, fallback, optional).
font-feature-settings - Controls advanced OpenType font features like ligatures, small caps, and old-style numerals.
font-variant-numeric - Controls numeric glyph alternates like tabular/proportional, lining/oldstyle.
line-clamp - Truncates text to N lines with ellipsis. Standard property (previously -webkit-line-clamp).
text-decoration-color - Sets the color of text decorations (underline, overline, etc.).
text-decoration-style - Sets the style of text decorations (solid, double, dotted, dashed, wavy).
text-decoration-thickness - Sets the stroke thickness of text decorations.
accent-color - Sets the accent color for form controls (checkboxes, radio buttons, range, progress).
color-scheme - Indicates which color schemes the element supports (light, dark, or both).
caret-color - Sets the color of the text insertion cursor in editable elements.
forced-color-adjust - Controls whether the element's colors adapt in forced-colors mode (auto, none).
mix-blend-mode - Sets how an element blends with its backdrop (multiply, screen, overlay, darken, etc.).
background-blend-mode - Sets how background layers (images, colors) blend with each other.
isolation - Creates a new stacking context, isolating blend modes from affecting parent elements.
mask - Shorthand for mask-image, mask-mode, mask-repeat, etc. Clips visible area using an image or gradient.
mask-image - Sets the image used as a mask layer (SVG, gradient, or image URL).
contain - Limits layout, style, and paint scope for performance (none, strict, content, size, layout, style, paint).
content-visibility - Skips rendering of off-screen content for performance (visible, hidden, auto).
contain-intrinsic-size - Provides estimated dimensions when content-visibility hides rendering.
appearance - Controls whether an element uses the OS-native styling (none removes it, auto uses platform defaults).
touch-action - Controls how touch gestures are handled (auto, none, pan-x, pan-y, manipulation).
scroll-snap-stop - Controls if snapping skips past snap points (normal) or stops at each (always).
border-collapse - Sets whether table borders are collapsed into a single border or separated (collapse | separate).
border-spacing - Sets the spacing between adjacent table cell borders (when border-collapse is separate).
table-layout - Controls the table layout algorithm. 'fixed' is faster and more predictable.
caption-side - Sets the position of a table caption (top or bottom).
empty-cells - Controls whether borders/backgrounds are shown on empty table cells.
outline-color - Sets the color of the outline. Useful for custom focus indicators.
outline-style - Sets the style of the outline (solid, dashed, dotted, double, etc.).
outline-width - Sets the thickness of the outline.
@media - Apply styles conditionally based on device/viewport characteristics (width, prefers-color-scheme, etc.).
@keyframes - Define the stages of a CSS animation by specifying styles at percentage waypoints.
@import - Import another stylesheet. Must appear before all other rules except @charset and @layer.
@font-face - Declare a custom font, specifying its name, source files, weight, style, and display behavior.
keywords.example: @font-face { font-family: 'MyFont'; src: url('font.woff2') format('woff2'); font-display: swap; }

keywords.description: @supports - Apply styles only if the browser supports a given CSS feature. Progressive enhancement.
@container - Apply styles based on a container's size (not the viewport). Modern component-level responsive design.
@layer - Declare cascade layers to control specificity order. Foundational for CSS architecture.
@property - Register a custom property with a type, inheritance, and initial value. Enables animating CSS variables.
@scope - Scope styles to a subtree of the DOM with optional lower boundary.
@starting-style - Define styles for the first frame of an element's appearance, enabling entry transitions.
var() - References a CSS custom property. Accepts an optional fallback value.
calc() - Performs math at render time. Can mix units (px, %, em, vw, etc.).
min() - Returns the smallest of the comma-separated values. Great for fluid sizing with a cap.
max() - Returns the largest of the comma-separated values. Sets a minimum effective size.
clamp(min, preferred, max) - Clamps a value between bounds. Fluid typography and spacing in one line.
env() - Accesses environment variables (e.g., safe-area-inset for notched devices).
rgb() - Defines a color using red, green, blue channels. Modern syntax supports alpha as 4th value.
hsl() - Defines a color using hue, saturation, lightness. Intuitive for creating color palettes.
oklch() - Perceptually uniform color space (lightness, chroma, hue). Best for consistent palettes.
color-mix() - Mixes two colors in a specified color space. Native color manipulation.
linear-gradient() - Creates a gradient image transitioning between colors along a straight line.
radial-gradient() - Creates a gradient radiating from a center point.
conic-gradient() - Creates a gradient rotating around a center point. Useful for pie charts.
url() - References an external resource (image, font, etc.) by URL.
fit-content() - Clamps a track size between its min-content and a max value. Used in grid.
minmax() - Defines a size range for grid tracks (minimum, maximum).
repeat() - Repeats a track pattern in grid layout. Works with auto-fill and auto-fit.
:hover - Matches when the user hovers over an element with a pointing device.
:focus - Matches when an element has received focus (click or tab).
:focus-visible - Matches when focus is visible (keyboard navigation). Preferred over :focus for outlines.
:focus-within - Matches when the element or any descendant has focus. Great for form highlighting.
:active - Matches while an element is being activated (mouse down, key press).
:visited - Matches links that the user has already visited.
:first-child - Matches the first child element within its parent.
:last-child - Matches the last child element within its parent.
:nth-child() - Matches elements by position (An+B formula). Supports 'odd', 'even', or formulas.
:nth-of-type() - Like :nth-child but counts only siblings of the same type.
:not() - Matches elements that do NOT match the given selector(s).
:is() - Matches any element matching one of the selectors. Reduces repetition. Takes highest specificity.
:where() - Same as :is() but with zero specificity. Ideal for defaults that are easy to override.
:has() - Parent/relational selector. Matches if the element's descendants match the argument selector.
:checked - Matches checked/selected form controls (checkboxes, radios, select options).
:disabled - Matches form elements that are disabled.
:enabled - Matches form elements that are enabled (not disabled).
:required - Matches form elements that have the required attribute.
:valid - Matches form elements whose content validates successfully.
:invalid - Matches form elements whose content fails validation.
:placeholder-shown - Matches an input when its placeholder text is visible (i.e., the input is empty).
:empty - Matches elements with no children (no elements, text, or whitespace).
:root - Matches the document root element (<html>). Commonly used to define CSS custom properties.
::before - Inserts generated content before the element's actual content. Requires content property.
::after - Inserts generated content after the element's actual content. Requires content property.
::placeholder - Styles the placeholder text of form inputs.
::selection - Styles the portion of text selected/highlighted by the user.
::first-line - Styles the first line of a block-level element.
::first-letter - Styles the first letter of a block-level element. Used for drop caps.
::marker - Styles the bullet or number of list items.
::backdrop - Styles the backdrop behind a fullscreen element or <dialog>.
grid-column-start - Sets where a grid item starts in the column axis.
grid-column-end - Sets where a grid item ends in the column axis.
grid-row-start - Sets where a grid item starts in the row axis.
grid-row-end - Sets where a grid item ends in the row axis.
grid-template - Shorthand for grid-template-rows, grid-template-columns, and grid-template-areas.
justify-items - Aligns all grid items along the inline axis within their cells.
justify-self - Aligns a single grid item along the inline axis within its cell.
subgrid - A grid-template-columns/rows value. Inherits the parent grid's tracks for alignment.
keywords.example: .card { display: grid; grid-template-rows: subgrid; grid-row: span 3; }

Dark mode with prefers-color-scheme and a manual toggle via data attribute

Small badge/tag component

items.name: inherit
items.description: Inherits the property value from the parent element

items.name: initial
items.description: Resets the property to its default/specification value

items.name: unset
items.description: Resets to inherit if the property inherits, otherwise to initial

items.name: revert
items.description: Reverts to the value from the previous cascade layer (user-agent/user stylesheet)

items.name: revert-layer
items.description: Reverts to the value from the previous @layer

items.name: currentcolor
items.description: Refers to the element's computed color property. Default for borders and outlines.

items.name: transparent
items.description: Fully transparent color (rgba(0,0,0,0))

items.name: none
items.description: No value / disable. Used in display, background, border, text-decoration, etc.

items.name: auto
items.description: Browser-calculated value. Meaning varies by property.

items.name: normal
items.description: Default/standard behavior. Used in line-height, font-style, white-space, etc.

items.name: px
items.description: Pixels — absolute, 1/96th inch. Good for borders and shadows.

items.name: rem
items.description: Relative to root font-size. Ideal for spacing and font-size.

items.name: em
items.description: Relative to parent font-size. Good for component-relative sizing.

items.name: ch
items.description: Width of '0' character. Perfect for text container max-width (65ch).
Percentage of parent dimension.

items.name: vw
items.description: 1% of viewport width.

items.name: vh
items.description: 1% of viewport height. Caution: ignores mobile URL bar.

items.name: dvh
items.description: 1% of dynamic viewport height. Accounts for mobile browser UI.

items.name: svh
items.description: 1% of small viewport height (browser UI visible).

items.name: lvh
items.description: 1% of large viewport height (browser UI hidden).

items.name: cqw
items.description: 1% of container query width. Used inside @container.

items.name: cqh
items.description: 1% of container query height.

items.name: fr
items.description: Grid fractional unit. 1fr = one share of remaining space.

items.name: lh
items.description: Relative to element's line-height.

items.name: block
items.description: Block-level box. Full width, new line.

items.name: inline
items.description: Inline box. Flows with text.

items.name: inline-block
items.description: Inline box accepting width/height.

items.name: flex
items.description: Flex container. 1D layout (row/column).

items.name: inline-flex
items.description: Inline-level flex container.

items.name: grid
items.description: Grid container. 2D layout (rows + columns).

items.name: inline-grid
items.description: Inline-level grid container.

items.name: none
items.description: Removed from layout and accessibility tree.

items.name: contents
items.description: Box disappears; children promoted to parent layout.

items.name: flow-root
items.description: Block box with new block formatting context (clears floats).

items.name: static
items.description: Default. Normal document flow.

items.name: relative
items.description: Offset from normal position. Creates positioning context.

items.name: absolute
items.description: Removed from flow. Relative to nearest positioned ancestor.

items.name: fixed
items.description: Removed from flow. Relative to viewport.

items.name: sticky
items.description: Scrolls then sticks. Hybrid relative + fixed.

items.name: white
items.description: #ffffff

items.name: orange
items.description: #ffa500

items.name: dodgerblue
items.description: #1e90ff

items.name: coral
items.description: #ff7f50

items.name: hotpink
items.description: #ff69b4

items.name: darkslategray
items.description: #2f4f4f

items.name: prefers-color-scheme
items.description: OS light/dark mode. Values: light, dark

items.name: prefers-reduced-motion
items.description: Reduced motion preference. Values: no-preference, reduce

items.name: prefers-contrast
items.description: High contrast preference. Values: no-preference, more, less, custom

items.name: prefers-reduced-transparency
items.description: Less transparency preference. Values: no-preference, reduce

items.name: min-width
items.description: Minimum viewport width for mobile-first breakpoints

items.name: max-width
items.description: Maximum viewport width for desktop-first breakpoints

items.name: hover
items.description: Hover capability. hover: hover (mouse), hover: none (touch)

items.name: pointer
items.description: Pointing precision. Values: none, coarse (touch), fine (mouse)

items.name: orientation
items.description: Viewport orientation. Values: portrait, landscape

items.name: display-mode
items.description: App display mode. Values: fullscreen, standalone, minimal-ui, browser

items.name: forced-colors
items.description: Windows high contrast mode. Values: none, active

items.name: color-gamut
items.description: Color range support. Values: srgb, p3, rec2020

items.name: ease
items.description: Default: slow start, fast, slow end

items.name: ease-in
items.description: Slow start, accelerates. For exit animations.

items.name: ease-out
items.description: Fast start, decelerates. For entry animations.

items.name: ease-in-out
items.description: Slow both ends. For looping animations.

items.name: linear
items.description: Constant speed. For progress bars and rotations.

items.name: cubic-bezier()
items.description: Custom curve: cubic-bezier(x1, y1, x2, y2)

items.name: steps()
items.description: Step timing for frame-by-frame animation
